issue_body:
Compilation fails on GCC with the `-fconcepts` flag. When building any code that includes `fmt/base.h` using an older GCC version (before 8.4) with `-fconcepts`, the compiler emits errors related to the `formattable` concept definition in `include/fmt/base.h`.

The issue appears to be that the library assumes full C++20 concepts support whenever any form of concepts is available, but older GCC versions with `-fconcepts` only provide the earlier Concepts TS (ISO-19217), which is not fully compatible with C++20 concept syntax.

Reproduction: compile any code that includes `fmt/base.h` with an older GCC using the `-fconcepts` flag. See https://godbolt.org/z/vv179vWGe for a live example.

Expected behavior: The library should compile cleanly when concepts are enabled, even on compilers that only support the Concepts TS rather than full C++20 concepts.
